stories.headline: A moment that changed me: I started yoga – and saw my scoliosis in a surprising new light
stories.summary: As a teenager I declined a painful operation to straighten my spinal curvature, and it was a decision I sometimes regretted. But through daily stretching and exercise, my relationship with my body was transformedI was 13 when a spinal surgeon gave me unsolicited career advice. “Scoliosis won’t ruin your life,” he…
stories.verification: Source pre-vetted (Tier 2 feed: The Guardian — Health)

stories.headline: Going to museums, movies, and theater may help your body stay younger
stories.summary: Going to the cinema, visiting museums, or attending concerts may do more than entertain. Researchers found that older adults who regularly participated in cultural activities tended to have bodies that functioned like those of people about three years younger. They believe stronger social ties, better mental health,…
stories.verification: Source pre-vetted (Tier 2 feed: ScienceDaily — Mind & Brain)

stories.headline: Scientists finally solved why some frogs survive a deadly fungus
stories.summary: A deadly fungus has wiped out amphibian populations around the globe, yet some mysteriously recover. Researchers discovered that survivors develop powerful immune defenses while they are still tadpoles, giving them a head start before the fungus can attack after metamorphosis. The study also uncovered a vast…

stories.headline: Chewing bubble gum after beetroot may help lower blood pressure
stories.summary: A new study found that chewing sugary gum after eating nitrate-rich vegetables or drinking beetroot juice helped the body produce more beneficial nitrite, leading to a temporary drop in blood pressure. Researchers hope the discovery will lead to healthier ways to enhance the cardiovascular and performance benefits of…

stories.headline: PAM Inhibitor Approved for HR-Positive Breast Cancer
stories.summary: (MedPage Today) -- The FDA approved the kinase inhibitor gedatolisib (Revtorpyk) for treating hormone receptor (HR)-positive/HER2-negative breast cancer in patients whose disease has progressed on endocrine therapy in the metastatic setting. Approval...

stories.headline: Novel Alzheimer's Drug Targeting Tau Shows Promise in Mid-Stage Trial
stories.summary: (MedPage Today) -- The tau-targeting agent diranersen did not meet the primary endpoint of the phase II CELIA trial, but that isn't stopping the drug from moving forward. The placebo-controlled study is the first to show a cognitive benefit and...

stories.headline: Experimental immune therapy shows promise against deadly childhood brain cancer
stories.summary: Several children who had aggressive recurrent brain tumors remained disease-free years after this treatment, according to an early-stage trial
stories.verification: Source pre-vetted (Tier 2 feed: Scientific American — Health)

stories.headline: Biomarkers for Esophageal Cancer Continue to Evolve
stories.summary: (MedPage Today) -- Biomarker discovery has played an essential role in the development of more effective therapy for multiple types of solid tumors, including esophageal cancer. The applicability of biomarkers in esophageal cancer continues to...

stories.headline: GLP-1s and Blinding Condition; Cyclosporiasis Source Found? Tylenol-Autism Lawsuits
stories.summary: (MedPage Today) -- Note that some links may require registration or subscription. A cohort study found that diabetes patients taking GLP-1 receptor agonists had a higher risk of anterior ischemic optic neuropathy, a potentially blinding eye condition...

stories.headline: Eating chili peppers may raise the risk of one deadly cancer
stories.summary: A major review found that people who consumed the most chili peppers had a substantially higher risk of esophageal cancer, though the evidence was less clear for stomach and colorectal cancers. Researchers emphasize that the findings show an association, not proof of cause and effect, and that more research is needed…

stories.headline: STAT+: Biogen Alzheimer’s drug slowed decline at rates on par with approved therapies, study shows
stories.summary: An experimental Alzheimer's drug from Biogen, one with a novel approach, slowed decline at comparable rates as approved drugs in a mid-stage study.

stories.headline: How to keep your cool when it feels like everything is going wrong
stories.summary: I don’t do well with stress. Even minor inconveniences can throw me off my game, turning a mildly bad day into a complete five-alarm fire. If someone asks a single thing of me during one of these spirals, I’m likely to blow up. Whether you’ve woken up on the wrong side of the bed or […]

stories.headline: Woman's Hour
stories.summary: We hear about a new study exploring the impact hormones have on women with ADHD.

stories.headline: 'Don't let blood be on your hands', mother tells inquiry
stories.summary: The mum of Elise Sebastian, who died in a mental health unit, gives evidence to the Lampard Inquiry.

stories.headline: Popular weight-loss drugs Ozempic and Wegovy may slow biological aging
stories.summary: Researchers found that semaglutide, the active ingredient in Ozempic and Wegovy, slowed biological aging markers in adults with HIV, marking the first clinical evidence that the drug may influence human aging. Although the findings are encouraging, scientists say larger studies are needed before concluding that the…

stories.headline: Is your period worsening your ADHD symptoms?
stories.summary: A first of its kind study by UK researchers is looking at the link between menstrual cycles and ADHD.

stories.headline: Scientists discover why peach fuzz can suddenly make you itch
stories.summary: A newly discovered network of fine hairs and specialized nerve cells appears to form a dedicated system for sensing mechanical itch, offering fresh insight into why chronic itching occurs. Because humans show signs of having the same pathway, the research could pave the way for more effective treatments for conditions…

stories.headline: Yale scientists found a hidden network inside the eye
stories.summary: Researchers have discovered that the retina uses an unexpected communication network that lets separate visual pathways cooperate instead of working alone. A newly identified "commander" cell appears to coordinate this system, helping the eye detect faint details that might otherwise be missed.

stories.headline: Exercise doesn't just strengthen the heart. It rewires it
stories.summary: Exercise doesn't just make the heart stronger. It also rewires the nerves that regulate it, a discovery that could pave the way for more personalized treatments for common heart conditions such as arrhythmias and angina.

stories.headline: How my period is supercharging my ADHD
stories.summary: A first-of-its-kind study by UK researchers is looking at the link between menstrual cycles and ADHD.

stories.headline: Study Dampens Hope for GLP-1s as Insulin Off-Ramp in Type 2 Diabetes
stories.summary: (MedPage Today) -- Adding a GLP-1 receptor agonist was not associated with a lower likelihood of discontinuing existing basal insulin therapy among veterans with type 2 diabetes compared with other glucose-lowering agents, a target emulation trial...

stories.headline: Alzheimer’s, stroke, depression: The preventative power of sauna
stories.summary: Sustained heat stress is bad for our health and can be deadly. But we’re discovering that heat therapies like sauna, when used in the right way, have surprisingly wide-reaching benefits for health
stories.verification: Source pre-vetted (Tier 2 feed: New Scientist — Health)

stories.headline: Losing just 80 minutes of sleep a night could make you gain weight
stories.summary: Sleeping about an hour and 20 minutes less each night for six weeks caused participants to gain weight and spend more time inactive. Researchers found that even mild, realistic sleep loss, similar to what many adults experience, had measurable effects. They warn that if this pattern continues over months or years, the…

stories.headline: Scientists discovered the brain doesn't make decisions the way we thought
stories.summary: A new study suggests the brain begins making decisions much earlier than scientists previously thought. Researchers found that even primary sensory regions are influenced by higher brain areas through rapid feedback loops, rather than simply passing information forward. This more dynamic view of brain function could…
stories.verification: Source pre-vetted (Tier 2 feed: ScienceDaily — Mind & Brain)

stories.headline: 2.6 million Americans lost health insurance in 2025 after ACA subsidies expired, leading to real health consequences
stories.summary: Research shows that losing coverage, even temporarily, tends to make people less healthy, and it exposes them to financial losses.
stories.verification: Source pre-vetted (Tier 1 feed: The Conversation — Health)

stories.headline: Why are healthy young non-smokers developing lung cancer?
stories.summary: An unexpected study found that young non-smokers with healthier diets had higher rates of lung cancer, raising questions about whether pesticide exposure from conventionally grown produce could play a role. Researchers stress that the findings are preliminary and require further studies before any conclusions can be…

stories.headline: Four children with terminal brain cancer saved by new cell therapy
stories.summary: An experimental immunotherapy has beaten aggressive brain tumours in a handful of children, and a personalised version is now being tested on more patients
stories.verification: Source pre-vetted (Tier 2 feed: New Scientist — Health)

stories.headline: Where you live could shape your dementia risk, massive study finds
stories.summary: Researchers analyzing data from more than 214,000 people found that dementia risk factors differ widely across countries, challenging the idea of a one-size-fits-all prevention strategy. At the same time, they uncovered surprisingly consistent patterns that could help shape smarter, more targeted public health efforts.
stories.verification: Source pre-vetted (Tier 2 feed: ScienceDaily — Mind & Brain)

stories.headline: Eat your ice cream for a long, healthy life? This doctor says so
stories.summary: Dr. Zeke Emanuel, a renowned U.S. health policy physician, takes on the wellness industry with a back-to-basics approach to optimizing good health.

stories.headline: Scientists discover a hidden heart valve risk linked to gum disease
stories.summary: Researchers found that bacteria linked to gum disease may help drive the development of calcific aortic valve stenosis by triggering inflammation and calcium buildup in the heart valve. The early findings suggest that keeping gums healthy could play a role in reducing the risk of this serious heart condition.

stories.headline: Alzheimer's tau protein has a surprising secret role in memory
stories.summary: Researchers found that tau is essential for turning new experiences into lasting memories by helping organize the brain's memory-storing cells. The mouse study also revealed how abnormal tau may contribute to Alzheimer's by disrupting both the formation of new memories and the recall of existing ones.
stories.verification: Source pre-vetted (Tier 2 feed: ScienceDaily — Mind & Brain)

stories.headline: Influencers are questioning common guidance about breast milk. Here's what science says
stories.summary: The guidance over leftover breast milk says toss it after 2 hours. But a recent preprint study shared on social media has some people questioning that advice.

stories.headline: Sniffing Chocolate Could Boost Your Leg Day Workouts and Curb Hunger
stories.summary: Smelling dark chocolate before and during workouts may help you perform more repetitions and reduce hunger after exercise, new research finds. Getty Images Smelling dark chocolate before and during a leg workout helped participants perform more repetitions. Though the exact mechanism remains unclear, researchers…

stories.headline: Intermittent Fasting Aids Weight Loss, With a Surprising Psychological Benefit
stories.summary: Researchers compared intermittent fasting with calorie counting and found that people following fasting protocols thought about food less often. stories.summary: A new study found that adults who slept about 78 fewer minutes each night for six weeks gained nearly a pound and became more sedentary, highlighting the important role sleep plays in maintaining a healthy weight. A woman lying on a bed. A new study suggests not getting enough sleep each night may lead to weight gain.…
